summary: Energetic first set with Jack Straw, Lazy>Supplication & Might As Well
blurb: The show delivers a punchy first set highlighted by Jack Straw, Lazy Lightning‑Supplication and a blistering Might As Well. Jerry’s lead work shines while Brent’s keyboards dominate the mix. The second and third sets keep the momentum with solid jams and an uplifting encore.
review: December 30, 1981 at Oakland Auditorium Arena captures the Dead in a high‑energy groove that leans heavily on a strong first set. Kicking off with Jack Straw, the band rolls into a tight run of They Love Each Other, On The Road Again and Dire Wolf before slipping into a classic Lazy Lightning → Supplication segue that many fans cite as a highlight. Cumberland Blues and a fiery Might As Well close the set with Jerry’s guitar in top‑shelf form and Brent’s keyboards filling out the sound, even if some mixes push his tone forward. The second set is more relaxed but still solid, leading into an inspired third set where Estimated Prophet blossoms into a driving Not Fade Away / Black Peter sequence before a rousing Johnny B. Goode. The encore’s Casey Jones wraps up the night on a high note. While recording quality varies—some SBD sources are treble‑heavy and bass‑light—the performance itself is consistently entertaining, making this a worthwhile listen for fans of the early‑‘80s era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise standout songs (Jack Straw, Lazy>Supplication, Might As Well) and Jerry’s lead work, with no consistent negative remarks about the band’s playing. Mixed comments focus on recording balance rather than performance, yielding a moderate positive sentiment.

best_recording.reason: Full‑show soundboard capture despite a treble‑heavy mix; contains all highlights and is the most referenced recording.

key_highlights: First set energy peaks with Jack Straw and the Lazy Lightning → Supplication sequence.
Blistering Might As Well close to the first set, showcasing Jerry’s lead work.
Cumberland Blues tight execution and solid third‑set jam on Estimated Prophet into Not Fade Away.
Encore Casey Jones delivers a classic finish.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers sweet, close‑to‑Jerry tone with shredding leads on Might As Well and responsive interplay during the Lazy → Supplication segue.
band_performance.Phil: Bass presence is occasionally masked by a treble‑heavy mix, but his foundation remains solid throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Provides steady rhythm work and tasteful slide accents, especially noticeable in The Other One references (though not on this setlist).
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard texture is prominent; some mixes push Brent forward while others note his early departure after the first set, leading to varied presence across tracks.
